summary: This is a brief memorial post published on September 11, 2004, the third anniversary of the 9/11 attacks. Joel Spolsky, based in New York City, posted a simple link to a memorial page. The post contains no analysis or argument — it is a solemn acknowledgment of the date's significance. Its brevity is itself the statement.
key_points: Published on the third anniversary of September 11, 2001
Contains only a single link to a memorial page, with no commentary
Demonstrates that sometimes silence and simplicity are more powerful than words
key_insight: Some moments call for silence rather than commentary, and a simple act of remembrance can be more powerful than any essay.
